hypothesis.statement: On the known-ahead final trading sessions of a calendar month/quarter, we test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned jointly on month-end-session membership AND proximity-to-RTH-close (the rebalancing window into the close) differs from ordinary sessions. This is a falsifiable rebalancing-flow conditioning hypothesis, not a profitability claim.
hypothesis.economic_rationale: Index funds, target-date funds, and mandate rebalancing concentrate buy/sell flow into the month-end and quarter-end closing auction window; whether this produces a measurable conditional forward pattern in the minutes into the close is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve.

new.name: is_month_end_session_flag
new.reason: Month-end / quarter-end session membership is DERIVABLE from the exchange trading calendar (last available trading session of the month/quarter) and known ahead; needs a new FeatureRequest extending SESSION_CALENDAR_ROLL. No month-end concept exists in the current SessionFeatureName enum. This is the SAME calendar-derivable flag declared (offline note) by the event-calendar month_end_flow card; the two cards must SHARE one FeatureRequest implementation rather than authoring two near-duplicate flags (REUSE-MAP rule).

lookahead_risk.description: Month-end / quarter-end last-session membership and minutes-to-close are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar and known ahead. The only hazard is mislabeling the last trading session when a holiday or early close shifts it; a naive calendar-day rule (last day-of-month) would be wrong.
lookahead_risk.mitigation: Derive the month-end / quarter-end flag from the ACTUAL exchange trading calendar (last available trading session in the month/quarter, accounting for holidays and EARLY_CLOSE sessions), carried as known-ahead point-in-time metadata under the _require_metadata_known_as_of guard so a holiday-shifted last session is never inferred from future data.

expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.statement: A calendar-derived month-end-x-close-proximity conditioner is structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms conditioned on. The most material non-orthogonality risk is internal to the seasonality set: overlap with the event-calendar month_end_flow card and redundancy with the existing day_of_week / session-position features, NOT overlap with the dead price-action factors.
expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.diagnostic_check: Residualize forward outcomes against the dead-mechanism factors (regime/vwap_session/bbo_tradability/liquidity_pa) AND against minutes_to_rth_close (to confirm the effect is not a generic into-the-close effect on all days). Cross-check against the event-calendar month_end_flow card to ensure the two are pre-registered as ONE pooled hypothesis, not double-counted (see FDR_BUDGET_PRIORITY_2_3.md de-duplication note).

capacity_turnover_note: Approximately 12 month-end + 4 quarter-end closing windows per year; low occurrence count, N_eff-limited. The quarter-end subset is expected to carry the strongest structural rationale but is the rarest. Pool across the index-futures family per Track-B. No capacity/turnover dollar claim.

data_dependency.detail: Month-end / quarter-end last-trading-session dates are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar; no external feed required. Same low-data-dependency class as opex_pinning and the event-calendar month_end_flow card, and shares its FeatureRequest implementation.

value_free_status.claims: hypothesis pre-registration only; no alpha/tradability/profitability claim
